review_notes: The control AC-4(19) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves validating metadata during information transfer, which can be enforced through NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and Service Meshes. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but a minor change was made for consistency. The functions map well to the described security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of 'enabled' to indicate the activation of a security feature.
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | validation of metadata when transferring informat
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(19) focuses on validating metadata during information transfer. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that metadata associated with network traffic and API requests is validated and enforced. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce rules based on metadata such as labels. Admission controllers can validate metadata on resource creation or updates. Service meshes like Istio can enforce policies based on metadata for service-to-service communication.
